assertions.title: Required command flags fail with deterministic validation errors

steps.expected: Structured JSON error with message about missing flags
Structured JSON error with message about missing flags

evidence.format: Structured JSON envelope with success=false, error.message containing fixed text

assertions.title: Invalid command-specific arguments surface as structured JSON errors

steps.expected: Structured JSON error envelope, not raw stderr
steps.observed: {"success":false,"data":null,"error":{"message":"invalid quality value \"invalid\": must be one of: 144, 450, 720"},"meta":{"command":"download","mode":"json"}}

evidence.format: Both returned structured JSON envelopes with success=false and error.message

assertions.title: Download range semantics are explicit and bounded

steps.observed: Code validates: start < 1 -> error, end > len -> error, start > end -> error with message 'invalid lecture range: start=%d end=%d (available 1-%d)'

steps.expected: Error for inverted range (would need valid auth)
steps.observed: "no lectures found" - cannot fully test without valid credentials
"no lectures found" - range validation occurs after API call
Tests confirm: 'start greater than end' -> 'invalid lecture range', 'end out of bounds' -> 'invalid lecture range', 'no lectures' -> 'no lectures found', default (0,0) -> full range

evidence.codeReview: internal/cli/cli.go:641-658 implements bounded range validation with deterministic error messages
evidence.unitTests: internal/cli/cli_test.go:345-362 covers inverted, out-of-bounds, and empty lecture list cases

assertions.notes: Live validation requires valid Impartus credentials. Code-level validation and unit tests confirm correct bounded behavior.

assertions.title: Extra positional arguments are either rejected or documented as accepted behavior

steps.expected: Either reject extra arg or document acceptance
Either reject extra arg or document acceptance

evidence.codeReview: internal/cli/cli.go:118-123 (version), 512-522 (serve) do not check for extra args; courses/lectures/download all have explicit len(args)/fs.NArg() checks

issues.description: 'version' and 'serve' commands silently ignore extra positional arguments, which could mask caller mistakes. Other commands (courses, lectures, download) properly reject them.
issues.recommendation: Either add positional argument validation to version/serve commands, or document this as accepted behavior in the manifest/help

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 3 passed (VAL-CLI-006, VAL-CLI-007, VAL-CLI-008), 1 failed (VAL-CLI-009). VAL-CLI-008 range validation confirmed via code review and unit tests (live testing requires credentials). VAL-CLI-009 reveals inconsistent handling: version/serve silently ignore extra args while courses/lectures/download properly reject them.
